stories.headline: February 2026: Largest AI Model Rush in History — 7 Major Models Drop Simultaneously
stories.summary: Gemini 3 Pro GA, Claude Sonnet 5, GPT-5.3, Qwen 3.5, GLM 5, DeepSeek V4, and Grok 4.20 are all launching in February 2026, making it the most competitive single-month LLM release window ever recorded.
stories.relevance: Directly impacts which model you choose for your accessibility app backend, RAG systems, and SaaS platform inference layer.

stories.headline: Claude 4.6 Opus Debuts with 1M Context Window and Agent Teams
stories.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6 featuring a 1 million token context window (beta), 128K output capacity, adaptive thinking, effort controls, and a new Agent Teams feature enabling multi-agent agentic workflows natively in the API. It is currently ranked #1 among proprietary LLMs on LogRocket's Feb 2026 AI dev tool power rankings.
stories.relevance: Critical for your real-time agentic systems project. Agent Teams pairs well with ADK/LiveKit orchestration you are already using.

stories.headline: DeepSeek Expands Context Window to 1 Million Tokens Ahead of V4 Launch
stories.summary: DeepSeek expanded its existing model's context window from 128K to 1 million tokens and is expected to release V4 (replacing V3) and R2 (successor to R1). DeepSeek's models continue to lead on math, coding, and logical reasoning benchmarks while remaining open-weight.
stories.relevance: Key model to benchmark for your local on-device inference experiments and RAG pipelines.

stories.headline: Alibaba Qwen3.5 Launches for 'Agentic Era' — Targets Multi-Step Autonomous Tasks
stories.summary: Alibaba released Qwen3.5, explicitly designed for agentic use cases: complex multi-step task execution, tool calling, and autonomous pipelines. It is a top-ranked open-source contender for February 2026.
stories.relevance: Strong candidate for your open-source agent stack — especially for RAG + tool-calling patterns in your SaaS cert platform.

stories.headline: GLM-5 Tops Open Source Rankings with 203K Context Window
stories.summary: GLM-5 by Zhipu AI (Z AI) was released under an open license with a 203K context window, claiming the #1 spot on the February 2026 open-source LLM leaderboard with top Quality scores across coding, reasoning, and multilingual tasks.
stories.relevance: A strong open-weight alternative for your smart glasses app where local inference matters.

stories.headline: Cursor 2.0 Launches with 8 Parallel Agents, Composer Model, and Plan Mode
stories.summary: Cursor 2.0 ships the new Composer model (4x faster than competitors), a redesigned multi-agent interface supporting up to 8 agents running in parallel, Plan Mode with editable Markdown plans, a visual editor bridging design and code, and enterprise features including shared transcripts, granular billing, and Linux sandboxing.
stories.relevance: Game changer for your full-stack SaaS development workflow — 8 parallel agents can handle frontend, backend, tests, and infra simultaneously.

stories.headline: Microsoft AI Toolkit for VS Code v0.30.0 — Adds OpenAI Response API + Multi-Agent Workflows
stories.summary: VS Code AI Toolkit February 2026 update (v0.30.0) adds support for models using the OpenAI Response API including gpt-5.2-codex, a new Workflow entry point for generating multi-agent workflows with GitHub Copilot, Qualcomm GPU recipes for edge/on-device inference, and interactive playgrounds for historical models.
stories.relevance: Directly useful for your multimodal and on-device inference projects — Qualcomm GPU recipes are especially relevant for smart glasses deployment.

stories.headline: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Kimi Code Enter AI Dev Tool Power Rankings
stories.summary: The February 2026 AI dev tool power rankings now include Claude Code (Anthropic's terminal-native coding agent), Gemini CLI (Google's command-line AI model interface), and Kimi Code (new entry from Moonshot AI), joining existing leaders like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, and Bolt.new.
stories.relevance: Claude Code is particularly aligned with your Python/FastAPI/Flutter workflow. Consider testing Gemini CLI for GCP-native workflows.

stories.headline: AlphaCode 3 Released — Contextual Debugging + Deployment-Ready Automation
stories.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaCode 3 builds on earlier versions with advanced contextual debugging, real-time pair programming, and deployment-ready automation trained on billions of lines of verified code — targeting complex framework-level development.
stories.relevance: Worth evaluating for your SaaS cert platform backend — especially for auto-generating boilerplate FastAPI and TypeScript modules.

stories.headline: Snyk Launches 'Agent Fix' — AI-Generated Security Patches That Auto-Retest
stories.summary: Snyk's 2026 release introduces Agent Fix: an AI model that generates security patches for vulnerabilities ac
